body:
## Summary
- Add `agent-update.yml` for near-biweekly Sepo infrastructure update checks using the existing `update-agent` skill.
- Add a pre-runtime pending update PR guard to avoid duplicate `agent/update-agent-infra-*` PRs, with a manual `force` override.
- Update docs, skill guidance, and focused workflow/script coverage.

## Tests
- `npm --prefix .agent run build`
- `node --test dist/__tests__/pending-update-pr-gate-shell.test.js dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js` from `.agent`
- `bash -n .agent/scripts/resolve-pending-update-pr.sh`
- YAML parse for `agent-update.yml` and `test-scripts.yml`
- `git diff --check`

Closes #27

comments.body:
Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `implement` -> `review` (round 3/10).

implementation succeeded; dispatching review

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777792797824 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUyNzI3NTY4NTI6aW1wbGVtZW50OjI3OnJldmlldzoxMTY6Mw -->

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 89 | PARTIAL | 8 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| workflow/generic | Validate delegated route authorization | pass | 8/8 | The new workflow is a GitHub `schedule`/`workflow_dispatch` entrypoint, passes `requested_by: ${{ github.actor }}`, and uses resolved GitHub auth before invoking the `update-agent` skill. |
| workflow/generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | `agent-update.yml` uses separate typed inputs for `source_repo`, `source_ref`, `update_skills`, `update_agent_md`, and `force` instead of overloading one command string. |
| coding/generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Changes are limited to the scheduled update workflow, pending-PR gate script, focused tests, and directly related docs/skill guidance. |
| coding/generic | Reuse existing code | partial | 6/8 | The PR reuses `scheduled-activity-gate`, `resolve-github-auth`, `resolve-agent-provider`, `setup-agent-runtime`, `run-agent-task`, and the existing `update-agent` skill, but misses the established `resolve-task-timeout` + `timeout-minutes` pattern for `run-agent-task`. |
| workflow/generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | The implementation matches issue #27’s requested default scheduled action, near-biweekly cadence, update skill reuse, and no duplicate/no-op PR behavior. |
| workflow/generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Updates `.agent/docs/actions/agent-actions.md`, `.agent/docs/architecture/supported-workflows.md`, `.skills/update-agent/SKILL.md`, and `README.md`. |
| workflow/generic | Surface stateful failures | pass | 7/7 | `resolve-pending-update-pr.sh` fails clearly on missing repo or empty branch prefix and emits structured skip/reason outputs rather than silently treating bad config as success. |
| coding_style/generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | The pending-PR gate is a small shell script using `gh pr list` and `jq`; the workflow composes existing actions without adding new runtime architecture. |

## Notes

- Main score driver: the PR fits issue #27 well and mostly follows established workflow composition patterns.
- The only rubric-specific follow-up is to add the standard task timeout resolver before the new `run-agent-task` step.

## Findings

- **WARNING:** `agent-update.yml` invokes `./.github/actions/run-agent-task` without the established `Resolve task timeout` step and without `timeout-minutes: ${{ fromJson(steps.task_timeout.outputs.minutes || '30') }}`. Existing workflow tests in `.agent/src/__tests__/envelope.test.ts` scan all workflow YAML files and require that contract for every `run-agent-task` step, so this weakens reuse of the shared runtime pattern and likely breaks the envelope test once the new workflow is present.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PARTIAL

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #116 implements issue #27, a child workstream from #107, by adding a default scheduled Sepo update workflow.
- The new workflow runs near-biweekly, supports manual dispatch inputs, invokes the existing `update-agent` skill, and skips when scheduling is disabled or a matching update PR is already open.
- The PR also adds a shell guard for pending update PRs, focused tests, and docs/skill guidance for the new maintenance path.
- Reviewers agree the overall design matches the request and reuses the right workflow/runtime pieces.

## Review
Overall judgment: the PR is structurally sound, but it has a few small workflow/test guardrail issues that should be fixed before merge.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Missing task timeout guard on update agent | WARNING | The new `run-agent-task` invocation does not include the shared task-timeout resolver pattern expected by newer workflow conventions. |
| Missing negative gate assertion for update workflow | WARNING | The new envelope test assertions cover the positive gate path but omit the matching guard that prevents step-level `steps.gate.outputs.skip` usage. |
| Gate result outputs use brittle echo writes | WARNING | The workflow writes `reason` outputs with bare `echo`, unlike the delimiter-safe helpers used by the gate scripts. |
| Pending update PR scan is capped at 100 | INFO | The pending-PR guard could miss an existing update PR in repositories with more than 100 open PRs. |

## Progress
- Issue #27 and parent context in #107 were reviewed; the PR matches the requested near-biweekly, non-spammy update workflow.
- The existing PR discussion already covers the timeout-resolver concern, so no duplicate inline timeout comment was posted.
- Inline comments were posted for the missing negative gate assertion and delimiter-safe output handling.
- Duplicated shell helpers were treated as intentional because these scripts run before runtime setup.

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>Missing task timeout guard on update agent</summary>

**Cause:** The new [`Run update agent`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/20ecaa3bfe5ba5b0f38cc6462ad76c63e7be022b/.github/workflows/agent-update.yml#L156) step calls `run-agent-task` without a preceding `Resolve task timeout` step or `timeout-minutes` wiring.

**Candidate solutions:** Add the standard timeout resolver before the step and set `timeout-minutes: ${{ fromJson(steps.task_timeout.outputs.minutes || '30') }}` on the `run-agent-task` step.

**Comments:** The rubrics review flagged this as the main consistency gap; the other review did not call it out, so this is treated as a warning rather than a blocker.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Missing negative gate assertion for update workflow</summary>

**Cause:** The new [`updateWorkflow` assertions](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/20ecaa3bfe5ba5b0f38cc6462ad76c63e7be022b/.agent/src/__tests__/envelope.test.ts#L204) mirror the positive scheduled-gate checks but omit the matching `assert.doesNotMatch` used for other scheduled workflows.

**Candidate solutions:** Add `assert.doesNotMatch(updateWorkflow, /if: steps\.gate\.outputs\.skip != 'true'/);` after the existing update workflow assertions.

**Comments:** Inline comment posted at `.agent/src/__tests__/envelope.test.ts:207`.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Gate result outputs use brittle echo writes</summary>

**Cause:** The [`Resolve gate result`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/20ecaa3bfe5ba5b0f38cc6462ad76c63e7be022b/.github/workflows/agent-update.yml#L87) step writes `reason` directly to `$GITHUB_OUTPUT` with `echo`, which can break if a future reason includes a newline.

**Candidate solutions:** Use the delimiter-safe output pattern already used by the shell gate scripts, or move this merge logic into a small script/helper.

**Comments:** Inline comment posted at `.github/workflows/agent-update.yml:98`.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Pending update PR scan is capped at 100</summary>

**Cause:** [`resolve-pending-update-pr.sh`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/20ecaa3bfe5ba5b0f38cc6462ad76c63e7be022b/.agent/scripts/resolve-pending-update-pr.sh#L74) uses `gh pr list --limit 100`, so a matching update PR beyond that window would be missed.

**Candidate solutions:** Raise the limit, use pagination if practical, or document the cap as an accepted low-probability edge case.

**Comments:** Low risk for this repository, so this should not drive automation unless maintainers want the extra hardening.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
`FIX_PR`: The remaining required work is a small set of deterministic workflow/test edits that is safe for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
`MINOR_ISSUES`

## Action Items
- [ ] Add the standard task timeout resolver and `timeout-minutes` wiring to the update workflow’s `run-agent-task` step.
- [ ] Add the missing `assert.doesNotMatch` guard for `updateWorkflow` in `envelope.test.ts`.
- [ ] Replace bare `$GITHUB_OUTPUT` `echo` writes in `Resolve gate result` with delimiter-safe output writes.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `review` -> `fix-pr` (round 4/10).

review verdict is minor_issues; dispatching fix-pr

- Addressed the PR #116 review items: delimiter-safe gate outputs, task-timeout wiring for the update agent step, and the missing negative gate assertion.
- Added the supporting task-timeout policy CLI/test coverage and documented `AGENT_TASK_TIMEOUT_POLICY`.
- Checks: `npm --prefix .agent run build`; focused Node tests from `.agent`; `bash -n .agent/scripts/resolve-pending-update-pr.sh`; workflow/action YAML parse; `git diff --check`.

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 8 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Validate delegated route authorization | pass | 8/8 | `agent-update.yml` is a scheduled/manual workflow entrypoint, runs through resolved GitHub auth, invokes `run-agent-task` with `route: skill`, and preserves audit context with `requested_by: ${{ github.actor }}`. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | Manual inputs are separate typed fields: `source_repo`, `source_ref`, `update_skills`, `update_agent_md`, and `force`; no overloaded command string is introduced. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | The diff stays scoped to the update workflow, pending-update gate, timeout wiring needed by workflow conventions, focused tests, and matching docs/skill guidance. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | The workflow composes existing shared actions including `scheduled-activity-gate`, `resolve-github-auth`, `resolve-agent-provider`, `setup-agent-runtime`, and `run-agent-task`; it also now uses the shared timeout resolver pattern expected by envelope tests. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | The implementation matches issue #27’s requested default scheduled action, near-14-day cadence, update skill reuse, PR-on-change behavior, and non-spammy no-duplicate guard; #107 context explains why #27 was selected instead of #94. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Updates `.agent/docs/actions/agent-actions.md`, `.agent/docs/architecture/supported-workflows.md`, `.agent/docs/customization/configuration-list.md`, `.skills/update-agent/SKILL.md`, and `README.md`. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Surface stateful failures | pass | 7/7 | `resolve-pending-update-pr.sh` fails clearly for missing repo or empty branch prefix, emits structured skip/reason outputs, and has focused shell regression coverage. Malformed timeout policy also returns a clear CLI error with tests. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | The pending-PR guard is a small shell script around `gh pr list` and `jq`; the workflow remains mostly composition of existing actions rather than new runtime architecture. |

## Notes

- The previous rubric warning about missing task-timeout wiring appears resolved in the current PR diff: `agent-update.yml` now has `Resolve task timeout` and `timeout-minutes` on `Run update agent`.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No selected-rubric failures found in the current PR diff.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #116 adds a default near-biweekly Sepo update workflow for issue #27, which was selected as the focused child task under parent issue #107.
- The workflow uses the existing `update-agent` skill, checks for existing update PRs before runtime setup, and only opens a PR when updates produce changes.
- The latest fix-pr pass also added task-timeout policy wiring, delimiter-safe workflow outputs, and the missing scheduled-gate test guard.
- Docs, skill guidance, shell/script tests, and workflow contract tests are updated alongside the runtime changes.

## Review
Overall judgment: the current PR head appears ready to ship. The prior required review items are fixed, and the latest review artifacts report either PASS/SHIP or only optional INFO notes.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| No actionable issues remain | INFO | Current reviews agree the required fixes are resolved. Remaining notes are optional cleanup, such as unused gate job outputs or low-risk defensive test expansion. |

## Progress
- Previous review items were addressed by fix-pr: timeout wiring, delimiter-safe gate outputs, and the missing `updateWorkflow` negative assertion.
- Current rubrics review is PASS with no rubric-specific follow-up.
- Claude’s latest review verdict is SHIP; Codex reports no BLOCKING, WARNING, or INFO findings.
- Existing inline comments are from the prior commit and are now covered by the current head; no new inline comments were needed.

## Recommended Next Step
`NO_AUTOMATED_ACTION`: No unresolved actionable branch-change work remains; optional INFO cleanup can be left to maintainer preference.

## Final Verdict
`SHIP`

## Action Items
- [ ] No required branch-change work remains.
@sepo-agent /fix-pr can we add an configurable variable for AGENT_AUTO_UPDATE and by default set to be true (but people can disable that)?

**Sepo pushed fixes for this PR.** Branch: `agent/implement-issue-27/codex-25272756852`. Requested by @lolipopshock.

Implemented `AGENT_AUTO_UPDATE` for PR #116. The scheduled `agent-update.yml` run now treats `AGENT_AUTO_UPDATE=false` as a disabled workflow override, while manual `workflow_dispatch` still works.

Updated docs/tests in the same pass:
- Documented `AGENT_AUTO_UPDATE` in README, workflow docs, config list, and update skill guidance.
- Added workflow contract assertions and a scheduled-activity test proving only automatic update checks are disabled.

Checks passed:
- `npm --prefix .agent run build`
- Focused Node tests from `.agent`
- YAML parse
- `bash -n .agent/scripts/resolve-pending-update-pr.sh`
- `git diff --check`
@sepo-agent /answer can users trigger it themselves? or they have to go to click the button in the actions?

Yes, but in the current PR “trigger it themselves” means GitHub’s manual workflow trigger, not an `@sepo-agent` comment command.

Current behavior:
- It runs automatically by default on the near-biweekly schedule: [`agent-update.yml#L3-L8`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/3f810d4f1d398bea4e98fd80e658913efd3fd4ca/.github/workflows/agent-update.yml#L3-L8).
- It also supports `workflow_dispatch`, so someone with permission to run repo workflows can start it from **Actions -> Agent / Update -> Run workflow**: [`agent-update.yml#L8-L32`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/3f810d4f1d398bea4e98fd80e658913efd3fd4ca/.github/workflows/agent-update.yml#L8-L32).
- They do not have to use the UI specifically; the same manual trigger can be invoked through GitHub CLI/API, for example `gh workflow run agent-update.yml`.
- `AGENT_AUTO_UPDATE=false` only disables the scheduled runs; manual dispatch remains available: [docs](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/3f810d4f1d398bea4e98fd80e658913efd3fd4ca/.agent/docs/actions/agent-actions.md#L49-L54), [config](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/3f810d4f1d398bea4e98fd80e658913efd3fd4ca/.agent/docs/customization/configuration-list.md#L21-L23).

So: no, users do not need to wait for the schedule; yes, they can trigger it manually if they have Actions permission. But this PR does **not** add an `@sepo-agent /update` style comment trigger.

title: Add scheduled Sepo update checks
